job_description:
We are looking for a senior, hands-on Agentic AI Solution Architect who can consult, design, and finalize the end-to-end solution architecture and scope of work for an enterprise-grade Agentic AI platform.
Location: DHA Phase 4 Lahore, Pakistan
Timings: 5pm-2am PKT (Fulltime, onsite)
This role is NOT about coding everything.
It is about:
Asking the right questions
Defining what should be built vs what should not
Designing a secure, scalable, cloud-native agentic architecture
Producing clear solution artifacts that engineering teams can execute on
What You Will Own (Scope of Work)
Business Discovery & Problem Framing
Work with stakeholders to:
Understand current processes, pain points, and automation opportunities
Identify where agentic AI truly adds value vs simple automation
Convert ambiguous ideas into:
Clear use cases
Agent responsibilities
Success metrics (accuracy, cost, latency, risk)
Deliverables:
Problem statements
Use-case prioritization
Agent responsibility matrix
Agentic AI System Design
Design multi-agent workflows including:
Task planning agents
Reasoning agents
Execution agents
Validation / guardrail agents
Decide when to use:
Deterministic workflows vs agent-driven flows
Human-in-the-loop vs autonomous execution
Deliverables:
Agent interaction diagrams
Decision trees & control flows
Cloud & AI Architecture (AWS-Centric)
You will design (not just recommend) the architecture using:
Core Stack
Amazon Bedrock
Model selection strategy (Claude, Nova, Titan, etc.)
Prompt orchestration & guardrails
Amazon Nova
Agent orchestration & reasoning layers
AWS Lambda
Event-driven execution
Tool calling by agents
Amazon Textract
Document ingestion & structured extraction
Amazon Bedrock Data Automation (BDA)
Knowledge grounding
Vectorization & retrieval
OpenAI models
Use-case based comparison vs Bedrock models
Hybrid model strategy (cost, latency, accuracy)
Deliverables:
High-level architecture diagram
Data flow & security model
Model selection rationale
Tooling, Integrations & Data Strategy
Define how agents will:
Call internal tools
Invoke APIs
Query databases
Handle documents & unstructured data
Design RAG vs Agentic Retrieval strategy
Define:
Prompt versioning
Memory (short-term vs long-term)
Context boundaries
Deliverables:
Tool invocation strategy
Data & memory architecture
Integration map
Governance, Security & Risk Controls
Define:
Role-based access
Prompt & output guardrails
Audit logging
Cost controls
Address:
Hallucination risks
Data leakage
Model drift
Deliverables:
AI governance checklist
Risk mitigation plan
Scope Definition & Delivery Blueprint
This is the most critical output.
You will:
Break the solution into phases
Phase 1: MVP
Phase 2: Scale
Phase 3: Autonomy & optimization
Define:
In-scope vs out-of-scope
Team roles required (Dev, MLOps, Cloud, QA)
Time & effort estimates (high-level)
Deliverables:
Final Scope of Work (SoW)
Phased roadmap
Delivery-ready architecture pack
Required Profile (Non-Negotiable)
Background
3+ years experience across:
Solution Architecture
AI/ML systems
Automation platforms
Has designed systems, not just implemented them
AI & Agentic Expertise
Proven experience with:
LLM-based systems
Multi-agent orchestration
Prompt engineering at scale
Strong understanding of:
When NOT to use agents
Trade-offs between agents, workflows, and APIs
Cloud & AWS
Deep hands-on knowledge of:
AWS (Lambda, IAM, S3, VPC)
Amazon Bedrock ecosystem
Event-driven architectures
Able to reason about cost, latency, and scalability
Business & Communication
Can:
Translate business problems into technical solutions
Push back diplomatically on unrealistic expectations
Communicate clearly with executives and engineers
Nice to Have (Strong Plus)
Previous consulting or pre-sales architecture experience
Experience designing AI CoE or platform teams
Familiarity with:
LangGraph / LangChain
Agent frameworks
Enterprise RPA + AI convergence
Experience in regulated or enterprise environments
Requirements added by the job poster
• 3+ years of work experience with Amazon Web Services (AWS)
• 2+ years of work experience with AWS Lambda

Job Summary:
The AI Engineer is responsible for designing, developing, and implementing AI/ML solutions that support business goals. This includes building machine learning models, integrating them into applications, optimizing existing AI systems, and collaborating with cross-functional teams to deliver scalable, data-driven solutions.
Job Responsibilities:
AI & Machine Learning Development
Design, build, and deploy machine learning models (supervised, unsupervised, NLP, deep learning).
Develop end-to-end ML pipelines including data ingestion, preprocessing, training, testing, and deployment.
Evaluate model performance using statistical and ML evaluation techniques.
Data Engineering & Processing
Collect, clean, and transform structured and unstructured datasets.
Work with data engineering teams to ensure high-quality data availability.
Build scalable data pipelines using tools like Python, SQL, Spark, or cloud-native services.
AI System Integration
Integrate AI/ML models into production systems (APIs, microservices, cloud platforms).
Work with engineering teams to operationalize models using MLOps practices.
Ensure CI/CD pipelines for model retraining and deployment.
Research & Optimization
Experiment with new algorithms, frameworks, and techniques.
Optimize models for performance, latency, and scalability.
Stay updated with the latest trends in AI/ML, LLMs, GenAI, and related technologies.
Collaboration & Documentation
Partner with business stakeholders to translate problems into AI-driven solutions.
Work closely with data scientists, data engineers, product teams, and architects.
Prepare documentation, technical reports, and solution designs
Job Requirements:
Strong experience in Python and ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, Hugging Face).
Hands-on expertise in building and deploying ML/AI models.
Understanding of LLMs, NLP, and generative AI concepts.
Solid foundation in algorithms, statistics, and probability.
Experience with cloud platforms (AWS / Azure / GCP).
Knowledge of MLOps tools (MLflow, Kubeflow, Docker, Kubernetes).
Experience with APIs and microservices.
Bachelor’s or Master’s in Computer Science, Engineering, AI, Data Science, or related field.
Certifications in AI/ML or cloud technologies. (preferred/good to have)
Experience with vector databases, embeddings, or RAG pipelines. (preferred/good to have)
Background in enterprise-scale AI solutions. (preferred/good to have)
Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.
Effective communication and stakeholder management.
Ability to work in cross-functional, agile teams.
Curiosity and continuous learning mindset.
Requirements added by the job poster
• Bachelor's Degree
• Working in an onsite setting
• 4+ years of work experience with AIML

About Turing
Turing is one of the world’s fastest-growing AI companies accelerating the advancement and deployment of powerful AI systems.
Turing helps customers in two ways: Working with the world’s leading AI labs to advance frontier model capabilities in thinking, reasoning, coding, agentic behavior, multimodality, multilinguality, STEM and frontier knowledge; and leveraging that work to build real-world AI systems that solve mission-critical priorities for companies.
Role Overview
We are seeking candidates with experience in any programming language or tech stacks to validate and ensure the quality of training data used in developing agentic AI systems. This role involves working with function-calling methods to create structured tasks, ensuring data workflows remain consistent, accurate, and aligned with cutting-edge AI requirements.
What does day-to-day look like?
Validate large-scale training datasets and maintain strict quality standards.
Create, manipulate, and structure JSON tasks for function-calling workflows.
Work closely with AI researchers and teams to align datasets with agentic AI use cases.
Monitor data accuracy, consistency, and integrity across projects.
Contribute to documentation and best practices for dataset preparation and validation.
Requirements
Strong proficiency in Python or JavaScript or Java or any other programming languages for data handling and workflow automation.
Solid understanding of SQL for querying and managing datasets.
Expertise or Knowledge in handling JSON.
Exceptional attention to detail with a focus on data quality and consistency.
Interest in or exposure to agentic AI, LLMs, or NLP best practices.
Bonus: Prior experience with data annotation tools or prompt engineering for Agent AI.
Perks of Freelancing With Turing
Work in a fully remote environment.
Opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI projects with leading LLM companies.
Offer Details
Commitments Required: At least 4 hours per day and minimum 20 hours per week with overlap of 4 hours with PST. (We have 3 options of time commitment: 20 hrs/week, 30 hrs/week or 40 hrs/week)
Engagement Type: Contractor assignment (no medical/paid leave)
Duration of Contract: 1 month (adjustable based on engagement)
Location: India, Pakistan, Nigeria, Kenya, Egypt, Ghana, Bangladesh, Turkey, Brazil, Mexico
Evaluation Process (60 mins) :
Below tests are setup in vetsmith for candidates to take it online at their convinient time
Automated coding challenge (Choose any programming language) - (30-45) mins
Function calling MCQ test (30 mins)
After applying, you will receive an email with a login link. Please use that link to access the portal and complete your profile.
Know amazing talent? Refer them at turing.com/referrals, and earn money from your network.
